attributes.description: An attributes object for a draw

callback_url.description: Optional HTTPS URL to receive a webhook POST when the draw completes or fails. Must be a valid HTTPS URL. Field included by default.

drand_randomness.description: The 64-character hex random value published by drand for the declared round. Combined with weather_value to derive the draw seed. Field included by default.

drand_round.description: The drand quicknet beacon round number used as entropy. Declared at lock time before the round resolves — the value is unpredictable at commitment time. Field included by default.

entries.description:
List of entries in the draw. Each entry must have an `id` field and optionally a `weight` integer for weighted draws. Entries may only be modified while status is `open`.

**Important — do not submit PII as entry IDs.** The entry list is hashed into a permanent, public proof record that cannot be deleted. If you use email addresses, names, or other personal data as IDs, you will be unable to honour a GDPR removal request without breaking the proof. Use opaque identifiers instead (e.g. a UUID or numeric ID from your own system) and keep the mapping from ID to person in your own database, where it can be deleted independently. Field included by default.

entry_hash.description: SHA-256 hash of the canonicalised entry list (JCS). Published at lock time as a cryptographic commitment. Changing any entry would produce a different hash. Field included by default.

metadata.description: Arbitrary JSON object stored with the draw. Not used in seed computation. Useful for storing external references (e.g. your internal draw ID). Field included by default.

name.description: Optional human-readable label for the draw. May only be set or changed while the draw is open. Field included by default.

results.description: Ordered list of winning entries, populated after draw execution. Each element mirrors the entry structure submitted. Field included by default.

seed.description: 64-character hex SHA-256 seed used to drive the draw algorithm. Derived from drand + weather entropy (seed_source: entropy) or supplied by the caller for manual draws (seed_source: caller). Field included by default.

status.description: Current lifecycle state of the draw. Progresses through: open → locked → awaiting_entropy → pending_entropy → completed. Terminal states: failed, expired. Field included by default.

weather_value.description: Temperature observation (°C) from the Middle Wallop, Hampshire Met Office station, taken at the declared observation time. Combined with drand_randomness to derive the draw seed. Field included by default.

winner_count.description: Number of winners to select. Must be between 1 and 10,000 and no greater than the number of entries. Field included by default.

relationships.description: A relationships object for a draw

draw-filter.description: Filters the query to results matching the given filter object

draw-filter-callback_url.description: Optional HTTPS URL to receive a webhook POST when the draw completes or fails. Must be a valid HTTPS URL.

draw-filter-drand_randomness.description: The 64-character hex random value published by drand for the declared round. Combined with weather_value to derive the draw seed.

draw-filter-drand_round.description: The drand quicknet beacon round number used as entropy. Declared at lock time before the round resolves — the value is unpredictable at commitment time.

draw-filter-entry_hash.description: SHA-256 hash of the canonicalised entry list (JCS). Published at lock time as a cryptographic commitment. Changing any entry would produce a different hash.

draw-filter-metadata.description: Arbitrary JSON object stored with the draw. Not used in seed computation. Useful for storing external references (e.g. your internal draw ID).

draw-filter-name.description: Optional human-readable label for the draw. May only be set or changed while the draw is open.

draw-filter-seed.description: 64-character hex SHA-256 seed used to drive the draw algorithm. Derived from drand + weather entropy (seed_source: entropy) or supplied by the caller for manual draws (seed_source: caller).

draw-filter-status.description: Current lifecycle state of the draw. Progresses through: open → locked → awaiting_entropy → pending_entropy → completed. Terminal states: failed, expired.

draw-filter-weather_value.description: Temperature observation (°C) from the Middle Wallop, Hampshire Met Office station, taken at the declared observation time. Combined with drand_randomness to derive the draw seed.

draw-filter-winner_count.description: Number of winners to select. Must be between 1 and 10,000 and no greater than the number of entries.

code.description: An application-specific error code, expressed as a string value.

detail.description: A human-readable explanation specific to this occurrence of the problem.

id.description: A unique identifier for this particular occurrence of the problem.

parameter.description: A string indicating which query parameter caused the error.

pointer.description: A JSON Pointer [RFC6901] to the associated entity in the request document [e.g. "/data" for a primary data object, or "/data/attributes/title" for a specific attribute].

status.description: The HTTP status code applicable to this problem, expressed as a string value.

title.description: A short, human-readable summary of the problem. It SHOULD NOT change from occurrence to occurrence of the problem, except for purposes of localization.

link.description: A link MUST be represented as either: a string containing the link's URL or a link object.

bearerAuth.description: API key issued by Wallop!. Pass as a Bearer token in the Authorization header.

info.description:
Provably fair random draw API. Every result is cryptographically committed before entropy is known, and permanently verifiable.

## Entry IDs and GDPR

**Do not submit personally identifiable information (PII) as entry IDs.**

The entry list is hashed into a permanent, public proof record that cannot be deleted without breaking the cryptographic proof. If you use email addresses, names, or other personal data as entry IDs, you will be unable to honour a GDPR removal request.

Use opaque identifiers instead — a UUID or numeric ID from your own system. Keep the mapping from ID to person in your own database, where it can be deleted independently of the Wallop! proof record.
